steps.title: Rewrite the cube root
steps.content:
A cube root is the same as raising to the $$\frac{1}{3}$$ power.
$$\sqrt[3]{5^5}=(5^5)^{1/3}=5^{5/3}$$

steps.title: Set the exponents equal
steps.content:
Both sides have base $5$, so their exponents must be equal.
$$6c=\frac{5}{3}$$

steps.title: Solve for c
steps.content: Divide both sides by $6$. $$c=\frac{5}{3}\cdot\frac{1}{6}=\frac{5}{18}$$ The answer is 5/18.
